title: Make the op-registry a faithful, attested, complete projection of the library: ctx through the op contract + full write-surface coverage + a parity test
context: FGAP-008/009/010 (and FGAP-006 as an instance): the op run(cwd, params) contract carries no DispatchContext, so cli/in-pi writes are attestation-blind (alpha); the registry omits library write capabilities (beta); nothing enforces parity across the library/op/script surfaces (gamma). The class is documented in analysis/2026-06-04-cli-op-registry-lossy-projection.md.
decision: (alpha) Add a writer/DispatchContext channel to the op-execution contract: OpDefinition.run gains a ctx/writer parameter, registerAll threads the pi ExtensionContext's verified identity, and the cli builds a DispatchContext from --writer for every write op — so attestation stamps uniformly rather than via per-op schema smuggling; context-archive's declared-but-ignored writer is fixed in passing. (beta) Complete the op surface against the library write set as dual-surface units (library + op + CLI + orchestrator script): add the missing removeRelation library primitive + a remove-relation / replace-relation op (closing FGAP-006), and ops for writeRelations / upsertItemInBlock / writeConfig / writeSchema+updateSchema / registerSubstrate+writeRegistry / writeBootstrapPointer+flipBootstrapPointer / bulk appendRelations as warranted. (gamma) Add a parity/coverage test that fails when a library write fn has no op or an op drops the ctx / optional params (dryRun, ordinal) its library fn or orchestrator-script sibling forwards; wire into husky + CI. The unified aim: the cli is a faithful, attested interface to everything one can do with pi-context out-of-pi.
consequences: The OpDefinition contract change ripples to all op handlers + registerAll + the cli dispatch + pi-workflows step-block (which must also thread ctx).
A new library primitive removeRelation + a batch of new ops; the op count grows toward library-write-surface parity.
The parity test becomes a standing guard: a new library writer without an op, or a ctx-dropping handler, fails the build.
FGAP-006 closes as part of beta; the append-block-item attestation instance closes as part of alpha.
Until landed, filing continues via the orchestrator scripts (which attest), not the cli.
